mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced factory utilization, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of CPIAUCSL growth above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of months where CPIAUCSL accelerates, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC = 0) over rolling 4-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and supports durable consumer demand, which lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where PAYEMS increases, or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between sluggish real GDP growth and falling personal consumption expenditures signals a demand-side recessionary shock, which reduces equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% and RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing activity and improving capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >62% of months where INDPRO accelerates, or the signal fails to maintain a positive hit rate above 54% across multiple market cycles.
